Resolves #11736

Realigns the deployment cookbook from active-#11720 wording to completed-MVP traceability, then records the first post-MVP deployment hardening ledger in the cloud-deployment Security guide. The ledger separates actioned MVP/runbook safeguards from tracked residual work for repo-clone credentials, platform secret management, network policy, container image hardening, and data-recovery evolution.

Evidence: L1 (docs-only static audit and local link existence check) -> L1 required (documentation close target). No residuals.

## Deltas from ticket
- Scope stayed to guide realignment and hardening review capture; no deployment code, compose, or Caddy runtime behavior changed.
- Container image hardening is recorded as deferred because the target production registry/runtime is not selected yet; the PR avoids inventing platform-specific controls without evidence.

### рџ”¬ Depth Floor

**Documented search (per В§7.1) вЂ” Rhetorical-Drift Audit of the Security.md hardening table:**

A security guide that misdescribes the mechanical posture is worse than no guide. I verified every factual "Current documented baseline" claim against `dev` (the PR changes only the 2 `.md` files, so the Caddyfile / compose I read are the exact reality the docs describe):

| Claim | Verification | Result |
|---|---|---|
| Caddyfile strips spoofable identity headers before optional auth injection | `ai/deploy/Caddyfile:30-41` вЂ” `route` block, `request_header -X-Preferred-Username` + `-X-Auth-Request-Preferred-Username` precede the (commented) `forward_auth`; header comment explicitly states stripping MUST run before the auth layer | вњ“ accurate |
| Deployment Cookbook Section 4 names the same invariant | `DeploymentCookbook.md:95-99` вЂ” "Section 4: Reverse Proxy and Auth Boundary вЂ¦ strips spoofable client identity headers, and injects the trusted identity headers" | вњ“ accurate |
| `NEO_KB_INGEST_TOKEN` is a tenant-scoped MCP credential, not a Git credential, not in `repoSlug`/logs | `HookWiring.md:37` вЂ” "Do not commit it, put it in `repoSlug`, or log it"; `:94` вЂ” "Bearer token for the repo-push automation identity"; `TenantIngestionModel.md:84,151` вЂ” secret-store sourced | вњ“ accurate |
| Compose keeps KB & MC internal via `expose`; `/kb/*` `/mc/*` public routing | `docker-compose.yml` вЂ” `kb-server`/`mc-server` use `expose:` (lines 59, 101); only `ingress` has host `ports:` (167); `Caddyfile:47-54` вЂ” `handle_path /kb/*`в†’`kb-server:3000`, `/mc/*`в†’`mc-server:3001` | вњ“ accurate |
| MVP compose provides per-service resource envelopes + readiness gates | `docker-compose.yml` вЂ” `resources: limits:` on chroma/kb/mc/ingress; `healthcheck:` on chroma/kb/mc | вњ“ accurate |

I also verified the 5 residual-sub descriptions newly added to the cookbook's owner map вЂ” #11731 (server-side repo-clone), #11732 (graph-store SQLiteв†’SQL), #11733 (downstream deployment-pipeline wiring), #11734 (local-model container profile), #11735 (tenant-source/parser deepening) вЂ” all OPEN, all faithfully summarized. Close-target #11736 confirmed not `epic`-labeled. Scanned both files for commercial-partner / client names вЂ” none; terminology is fully generic.

**Challenge (per В§7.1) вЂ” two non-blocking follow-up concerns:**

1. **Residual list will need a #11766 line.** The cookbook's "Post-MVP residual work" map enumerates #11730вЂ“#11736 but not **#11766** (fold-the-swarm-heartbeat-into-the-Orchestrator), filed as a #11730 sub ~35 min after this PR opened. Genuinely non-blocking вЂ” the PR predates #11766, and the PR's own closing line ("Future guide drift should be handled as focused follow-ups") is exactly the right disposition. The #11766 implementation PR should add the cookbook line as part of its own scope.
2. **`Container image hardening` row defers without a tracking ticket.** AC2 phrases dispositions as "actioned **or** ticketed"; that row is "recorded + deferred, file a follow-up when the registry/runtime is chosen." I judge this **acceptable, not a Required Action**: the finding is permanently recorded in the security guide (not lost), the deferral rationale is sound per truth-in-code (platform-specific controls вЂ” non-root, read-only rootfs, signing, SBOMs, scanning вЂ” can't be meaningfully ticketed without a chosen platform; a ticket now would be a hollow placeholder), and #11736 is itself a defer-with-rationale workstream. Flagging it transparently so the AC-compliance reasoning is on the record.

**Rhetorical-Drift findings:** Pass. One micro-note (not actioned): the `NEO_KB_INGEST_TOKEN` cell extends the guide-tree's documented "not committed / not in repoSlug / not logged" to "вЂ¦manifests, or graph-visible config" вЂ” a sound generalization of the same don't-expose-secrets principle, no false-security claim, no reader misled. The `#11733` disposition is used as the catch-all home for platform-runtime hardening (K8s netpol, KMS); #11733 is titled "deployment-pipeline wiring" вЂ” the closest existing residual sub, and the PR's "once a concrete platform is selected" framing makes it a defensible home. Neither rises to a finding.
